---
title: "Gaussian Spike Model Fit With GSE164378 3' CITE-seq"
output: html_document
---

This tutorial checks the Gaussian spike model fit on the RNA side of the
package GSE164378 3' CITE-seq example. The example keeps 4,000 matched PBMC
cells, 2,000 RNA HVGs, and all 228 ADT features.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(scScale)
data(gse164378_3p_citeseq_hvg)

counts <- gse164378_3p_citeseq_hvg$rna_counts
adt <- gse164378_3p_citeseq_hvg$adt_counts
```

## Fit Spike Model

```{r fit-spike-model}
fit <- scscale_fit(
  counts,
  r = 20,
  fit_umi = FALSE,
  mp_max_iter = 20,
  mp_grid_n = 4000
)

data.frame(
  p = fit$p,
  n = fit$n,
  c_X = fit$c_X,
  selected_iteration = fit$bulk$selected_iteration,
  stop_reason = fit$bulk$stop_reason,
  tau2 = fit$bulk$tau2,
  lambda_plus = fit$bulk$lambda_plus
)

fit$bulk$history[, c(
  "iteration", "n_bulk", "tau2", "lambda_plus", "n_spikes",
  "qq_rmse_log", "ks", "stop_metric_value"
)]

data.frame(
  rank = fit$spikes$rank,
  lambda = fit$spikes$lambda,
  q_X = fit$spikes$q_X,
  theta_X = fit$theta_X,
  theta_infinity = fit$theta_infinity
)
```

## Eigenvalue Fit

```{r plot-spike-fit, fig.width=11, fig.height=3.7}
lambda <- fit$spectrum$lambda
bulk <- fit$bulk
bulk_lambda <- lambda[bulk$bulk_index]
bulk_lambda <- bulk_lambda[is.finite(bulk_lambda) & bulk_lambda > 0]
mp_density <- getFromNamespace("scscale_mp_density", "scScale")
mp_quantile <- getFromNamespace("scscale_mp_quantile", "scScale")

mp_x <- seq(bulk$mp_lower, bulk$lambda_plus, length.out = 600)
mp_y <- mp_density(mp_x, c_X = fit$c_X, tau2 = bulk$tau2)

old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.4, 1))
hist(
  bulk_lambda,
  breaks = 60,
  freq = FALSE,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "white",
  xlab = expression(lambda),
  main = "Bulk eigenvalues"
)
lines(mp_x, mp_y, col = "#D55E00", lwd = 2)
abline(v = bulk$lambda_plus, col = "#D55E00", lty = 2)

hist(
  log10(bulk_lambda),
  breaks = 60,
  freq = FALSE,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "white",
  xlab = expression(log[10](lambda)),
  main = "Log bulk eigenvalues"
)
abline(v = log10(bulk$lambda_plus), col = "#D55E00", lty = 2)

emp_q <- sort(bulk_lambda)
theory_q <- vapply(
  ppoints(length(emp_q)),
  mp_quantile,
  numeric(1),
  c_X = fit$c_X,
  tau2 = bulk$tau2,
  grid_n = 4000
)
plot(
  theory_q,
  emp_q,
  pch = 16,
  cex = 0.45,
  col = "#0072B2",
  xlab = "MP theoretical quantile",
  ylab = "Empirical bulk quantile",
  main = "Bulk QQ"
)
abline(0, 1, col = "#D55E00", lwd = 2)
par(old_par)
```
